content: The purposeful manipulation in the heredity of crops and the production of new superior varieties of existing crop plants is called ___________
explanation:
The correct answer is:

✅ Option D — plant breeding

Explanation:

Plant breeding is the purposeful manipulation of plant heredity to develop new and improved varieties with desirable traits such as higher yield, disease resistance, and better nutritional quality.

content:
Match a type of artificial method of vegetative reproduction given in Column I with examples given in Column II.

Column (I) Column (II)
i. Root cutting a. Apple
ii. Bud grafting b. Sansevieria
iii. Stem cutting c. Blackberry
iv. Leaf cutting d. Rose

Choose the correct option:

options.content: i - c, ii - d, iii - a, iv - b
i - c, ii - b, iii - d, iv -a
i - c, ii - a, iii - d, iv - b
i - a, ii - d, iii - c, iv - b

content:
Match the Column I containing mutant varieties with Column II containing respective crop plants.

Column I
 Column II
i. Jagannath a. Cotton
ii. NP- 836 b. Cabbage
iii. Indore - 2 c. Rice
iv. Regina - II d. Wheat

Choose the correct option from below.

options.content: i - c ii - d iii -a iv - b

explanation:
Let's match the mutant varieties with their respective crop plants:

  • i. Jagannath: This is a well-known mutant variety of Rice.

  • ii. NP-836: This is a mutant variety of Wheat.

  • iii. Indore - 2: This is a mutant variety of Cotton.

  • iv. Regina - II: This is a mutant variety of Cabbage.

Now, let's map these to the options provided:

  • i. Jagannath → c. Rice

  • ii. NP-836 → d. Wheat

  • iii. Indore - 2 → a. Cotton

  • iv. Regina - II → b. Cabbage

Comparing this with the given options:

Option D: i - c, ii - d, iii - a, iv - b

This matches our findings.

The final answer is $\boxed{D}$

content: The purposeful manipulation in the heredity of crops and the production of new superior varieties of existing crop plants is called _________
explanation:
The correct answer is:

Option D: plant breeding ✅

Explanation:

  • Genetics → It is the study of heredity and variation, not directly the production of new crop varieties.

  • Traditional biotechnology → Includes ancient practices like fermentation, not specifically crop improvement.

  • Horticulture → It is the branch of agriculture dealing with fruits, vegetables, flowers, etc., not necessarily with hereditary manipulation.

  • Plant breeding → This is the purposeful manipulation in the heredity of crops to produce new superior varieties (like higher yield, disease resistance, better quality).

Hence, the answer is plant breeding.
